title: chamaecytisus virescens
intro: Chamaecytisus virescens, also known as green broom or green broom shrub, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to Eastern Europe, Western Asia, and the Caucasus region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as dyspepsia and diarrhea, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Chamaecytisus virescens, often in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, have been used to alleviate symptoms of stomach ulcers, gastritis, and other gastrointestinal disorders.
